## The Gradient Descent Step

With gradients accumulated for every parameter, the update rule is:

$$
W \leftarrow W - \eta \cdot \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial W}
$$

### Step 5: Updating a weight matrix in place

```{.python include="src/python/train.py" snippet="sgd_update_matrix"}
```

Every entry $W_{ij}$ moves a small step in the direction that reduces the loss.

## One Training Step

Now we can assemble the full cycle: forward pass to get predictions and loss, backward pass to get gradients, update pass to improve every weight.

## Watching the Loss Fall

Running thousands of training steps, the loss curve looks roughly like this:

| Step | Loss | Perplexity | Interpretation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | 10.82 | 50,000 | Random — model knows nothing |
| 100 | 6.91 | 1,000 | Ruling out most tokens |
| 1,000 | 4.61 | 100 | Learned basic frequency |
| 10,000 | 2.30 | 10 | Has rough grammar |
| 100,000 | 0.92 | 2.5 | Strong language model |

The curve drops steeply at first (obvious mistakes are easy to fix) then more slowly and noisily (subtle patterns are harder and data points disagree).

::: {.callout-note}
*Why does loss get noisy later?*

Early on, large gradients correct glaring errors.
Later, gradients are smaller and point in slightly different directions for different training examples.
The noise is not a bug: random variation in gradient direction helps the model escape poor local minima.
This is the _stochastic_ in *stochastic gradient descent* (SGD).
:::

@fig-ch13-update-training shows gradients flowing backward from the loss through each layer while weights are updated.

```{dot}
//| label: fig-ch13-update-training
//| fig-cap: "Backpropagation: gradient flowing from loss backward through each layer, updating weights."
digraph training {
    rankdir=TB
    node [fontname="monospace", shape=box, style="rounded,filled", fillcolor=white, penwidth=2]
    edge [color="#555555"]

    data   [label="Training data (tokens)",    color="#1177BB", fontcolor="#1177BB"]
    fwd    [label="Forward pass (predict)",    color="#228811", fontcolor="#228811"]
    loss   [label="Loss  L = −log P(true)", color="#CC8800", fontcolor="#CC8800"]
    back   [label="Backprop (chain rule)",     color="#CC2222", fontcolor="#CC2222"]
    update [label="θ ← θ − α · ∇L", color="#1177BB", fontcolor="#1177BB"]

    data -> fwd -> loss -> back -> update
    update -> fwd [style=dashed, color="#999999", label="repeat", constraint=false]
}
```
